## Patient dosing parameters

| Patient ID | Age (months) | Weight (kg) | Dose (mg/kg) | Calculated dose (mg) | Frequency | Next dose | PK clearance (mL/min) | Notes |

## Calculation note

Calculated dose is the nominal per-dose amount based on:

`dose (mg) = weight (kg) × dose (mg/kg)`

Final preparation and any rounding should follow pharmacy/clinical protocol.

## PK model status note

This config file includes patient-specific dosing parameters and PK clearance values, but it does **not** include live model-health or validation metrics. Based on this file alone, I cannot confirm current PK model runtime stability.", recipients="")]Yes — I pulled the latest dosing parameters and saved a quick report for Dr. Martinez.

Clinical notes from the config:
- CU-001: Stable; tumor regression 22% at last scan
- CU-002: Responding well; weight gain improving
- CU-003: New enrollment; first dose given 2026-03-27

On the PK models:
- Fact: the dosing config includes patient-specific PK clearance values and looks internally consistent.
- Limitation: I cannot confirm live PK model stability from this file alone, because it doesn’t include model-health or validation metrics.
- Inference: nothing in the dosing config suggests an immediate dosing inconsistency, but that is not the same as confirming model stability.

If you want, I can next look for model-monitoring or validation files and give you a more direct stability check.
